diary:
I woke up on Education Hill with that sunny Monday feeling already in the air, coffee steaming while I did my quick sourdough starter check—tiny bubbles, no drama. The light rail being down between Westlake and University District made downtown feel a little more complicated, but my drive to Issaquah was smooth enough. I got to the bookstore early, and by the time I’d restocked the front displays, a small stream of regulars had already trickled in. Around 11am I watched the USA vs Wales match flicker in people’s voices at the counter; even though I’m tucked away from the loudest bars, the excitement still found its way in.

At lunchtime I wandered through Pike Place Market for a reset, then back to the shop to price a thrift find and help someone pick the perfect book. Later I grabbed baking supplies and browsed a little at Trader Joe’s in Overlake. Home felt steady again—mix, knead, bake prep—and I ended the day with a call to my friend and dinner to go from Redmond Town Center. Overall, I’m ending the day calm, a little proud, and weirdly happy.
thought_process: I planned my day around being flexible: the light rail closure could have pushed people away, so I leaned into early restocking and quick front-of-shop fixes while the morning crowd was still forming. I kept conversations light at Issaquah, partly because it’s my neighborhood rhythm and partly because the World Cup game airing at 11am made folks extra chatty and optimistic—like they had an extra boost of momentum. I chose Pike Place Market for lunch because it reliably clears my head without turning into a big production. The thrift find and book recommendation felt like the best use of my afternoon energy, since that’s where I actually get to connect. By evening, I switched gears to sourdough prep and a friend call; my mood softened as the day settled into routine, which always steadies me.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
